text.content:
SINGING A VERSE

When you sing a verse, you combine a volume, a key and a tone you learned. These three aspects determine the verse's MP cost, targets and effect.

  • The volume determines the sound intensity of your verse (and the creatures you can reach with it).

  • Each tone determines the general effects of your verse.

  • Each key has an associated damage type, status effect, Attribute and recovery. These modify the effects determined by the tone of your verse.

Example: you might combine a low volume with the key of Flame and a haunting tone to inflict shaken on a single creature for 10 Mind Points, or you could combine a medium volume with the key of Iron and an energetic tone to allow your allies to make additional progress on Clocks when using Willpower.

VOLUME LIST

Volume

MP

Targets

Low

10

Yourself or another creature you can see who can hear you.

Medium

20

Every ally who can hear you.

High

30

Every enemy who can hear you.

Recovery

CALM TONE

@UUID[Compendium.projectfu.skills.Item.XCtijox9eBrBNSno]{Calm Tone}

Each target recovers 【key recovery】 equal to 【10 + twice your current Willpower die size】. This amount increases by 10 if you are level 20 or higher, or by 20 if you are level 40 or higher.

If this tone causes MP recovery, it has no effect on the character who sings it.

ENERGETIC TONE

@UUID[Compendium.projectfu.skills.Item.XvkMOHPXUg0ikDkf]{Energetic Tone}

Until the start of your next turn, when a target succeeds on a Check that includes 【key Attribute】 and that Check allows them to advance or turn back a Clock, they may fill or erase an additional section of that Clock.

FRANTIC TONE

@UUID[Compendium.projectfu.skills.Item.mtKts9zjHlweSVk4]{Frantic Tone}

You deal an amount of 【key type】 damage equal to twice your current Willpower die to each target. This tone deals 5 extra damage if you are level 20 or higher, or 10 extra damage if you are level 40 or higher.

HAUNTING TONE

@UUID[Compendium.projectfu.skills.Item.CynydNkxJS1HZVlJ]{Haunting Tune}

Each target suffers 【key status effect】. Each target also loses Resistance to 【key type】 damage (if they have it) until the start of your next turn, and cannot gain Resistance to that damage type.

LIVELY TONE

@UUID[Compendium.projectfu.skills.Item.zLxqETSi6zbRkWKu]{Lively Tone}

Each target treats their 【key Attribute】 as being one die size higher (up to a maximum of d12) until the start of your next turn.

MENACING TONE

@UUID[Compendium.projectfu.skills.Item.8QMh3jUtgmIMy9Jb]{Menacing Tone}

The first time each target suffers damage before the start of your next turn, that damage becomes 【key type】. This can trigger once for each target of the verse, separately.

SOLEMN TONE

@UUID[Compendium.projectfu.skills.Item.yidpNKJQMA7dSBsc]{Solemn Tone}

Each target recovers from 【key status effect】. Each target also gains Resistance to 【key type】 damage until the start of your next turn.

1. To automate class feature, add the following item to your character sheet: @UUID[Compendium.projectfu.skills.Item.nu7QnUu7tmVv6zxU]{Verse}

  2. A full list of keys and tones can be found here: @UUID[.W4kQjYXrLRVomFdg]{KEY LIST} and @UUID[.mm3q742jIIhvWG1U]{TONE LIST}.

  3. Open the verse sheet and navigate to "Details" tab

  4. There are two dropdowns, select a key followed by a tone.

  5. To start singing, roll the Verse feature and combine a key, tone and volume in the dialog menu.
